additions.template: what's on my to do list
what do i have to do
how many tasks do i have
delete {description:text} from my to do list
get rid of all my tasks
do i have anything due today
what do i have to do today
what needs to get done today

gaps.want: delete the first task / delete task 3 / delete the last task i added
gaps.needs: positional or recency-based selection in delete-task; skill currently matches by exact description only (a {index:quantity} input plus DSL support for indexed read)

gaps.want: mark the milk one as done / delete the report thing (partial description)
gaps.needs: fuzzy or contains matching in read_one; delete-task and complete-task match description exactly, so any utterance with articles or partial wording fails at execution even when routed correctly

gaps.want: what's due tomorrow / what's due this week / what's due by friday
gaps.needs: due-tasks takes no inputs and hard-codes today+overdue; needs an optional {dueDate:date} or range input and skill logic

gaps.want: add buy milk, call mom, and gym to my list (multiple tasks at once)
gaps.needs: list splitting in create-task; one template would create a single task with the whole string as description

gaps.want: what did i get done today / show my completed tasks
gaps.needs: a list-completed-tasks skill (none exists; list-tasks filters completed out)
not addable as a template safely: 'i did' collides with activity logging (log-run/log-walk style utterances such as 'i did a 5k'); would need router-level disambiguation or fuzzy fallback

notes: complete-task skill EXISTS (checked v0/data/skills/complete-task.json) so 'mark X done' variants are template additions, not gaps. ORDERING: (1) all clear-tasks literals must precede delete-task templates so 'delete all my tasks' / 'remove everything from my list' never binds a delete-task description slot; (2) 'mark the reminder to X as done' (complete-reminder) must precede 'mark the task X as done' and the existing broad 'mark X as done'; likewise 'mark the task X as/done' must precede 'mark X as/done' or the description captures 'the task ...' and exact-match lookup fails; (3) date-bearing create-task templates ('add a task to X due D', 'add X due D', 'remind me to X by D', 'i need to X by D') must precede their date-less counterparts and the existing broad 'remind me to {description}' must remain LAST among all remind-me templates (after all reminder skills and task-before-birthday); (4) due-tasks "what's on my plate today" and 'what do i have to do today' must precede list-tasks "what's on my plate" / 'what do i have to do'. OVER-MATCH: '{description} is done' is the weakest-anchored addition (trailing anchor only) - keep it last in complete-task ordering; 'i have to X' / 'i gotta X' follow the precedent of the existing 'i need to X' but will catch some venting-style utterances; 'note to self X' is assigned to create-task but some users mean a journal entry; 'take X off my list' is mapped to delete-task though a minority of speakers mean 'completed' - deletion is destructive, consider routing to complete-task instead if soft-delete semantics matter. Terse literals 'tasks' and 'my tasks' assume full-utterance anchored matching (safe only if the router anchors templates at both ends).
